title: 10 best herbal capsules for motion sickness
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for motion sickness are natural supplements designed to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness, a condition characterized by nausea, dizziness, and vomiting triggered by travel or motion. The best herbal capsules for motion sickness include Zingiber officinale, Ginkgo biloba, Passiflora incarnata, Avena sativa, Piper nigrum, Cinchona officinalis, Foeniculum vulgare, Centella asiatica, Glycyrrhiza glabra, and Uncaria rhynchophylla. If left untreated, motion sickness can lead to missed work, cancelled trips, and strained relationships, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain bioactive compounds that work in harmony to alleviate symptoms. The capsaicin present in ginger has a natural anti-inflammatory effect, which soothes the stomach and reduces nausea. Additionally, the gingerols and shogaols in ginger have been shown to inhibit the activity of serotonin receptors, which can contribute to motion sickness. By targeting these underlying causes, herbal ginger capsules provide quick relief from dizziness, vomiting, and stomach discomfort associated with motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Ginger's Anti-Inflammatory Compound: Ginger contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

5-Hydroxytryptamine (5-HT3) Receptor Blockade: Ginger has been shown to block the action of 5-HT3 receptors in the gut, which helps alleviate nausea and vomiting caused by motion sickness by reducing the transmission of serotonin signals from the gut to the brain.

Vasodilation and Increased Blood Flow: Herbal ginger capsules can help increase blood flow and dilate blood vessels, which may help reduce the symptoms of motion sickness, such as dizziness and lightheadedness, by improving oxygenation of the body's tissues.
remedy_constituents: Shogaol: Shogaol is a ginger-derived compound that helps alleviate motion sickness by inhibiting the activity of dopamine receptors, which are involved in the regulation of nausea and vomiting.

Gingerols: Gingerols, particularly 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol, are bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce the symptoms of motion sickness by inhibiting the release of pro-inflammatory mediators and protecting against oxidative stress.

Gingerol analogs (such as zingerone): Gingerol analogs, such as zingerone, have been shown to have a stabilizing effect on the stomach and small intestine, which can help alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness by reducing the frequency and severity of nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Sliced or dried rhyzomes are used to make capsules for motion sickness because of their high gingerol and shogaol content, which have anti-inflammatory and nausea-relieving properties.
Roots: Dried ginger roots are used to make capsules for motion sickness because of their ability to reduce nausea and vomiting by inhibiting the activity of the stomach's digestive enzymes.
Barks: Some products might use dried barks to create a capsule, but it's less common and still used for its ability to provide relief from nausea and vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes in the morning for optimal potency.
Clean the rhizomes with distilled water and scrub them gently with a soft brush for 10 minutes.
Dry the rhizomes in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 24 hours or until they reach 8% moisture content.
Grind the dried rhizomes into a fine powder using a coffee grinder with 50 grams per batch and 30 seconds grinding time.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of zingiber officinale powder each and seal them immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain peppermint oil, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to ease stomach discomfort and reduce nausea. When taken before travel or during a period of motion, the capsules work to soothe the digestive system and calm the body's reaction to the physical movements. This helps to alleviate symptoms such as dizziness, lightheadedness, and queasiness, making it easier for individuals to enjoy their journey without discomfort.
remedy_properties: Menthol content: Peppermint's menthol content helps to stimulate digestive enzymes, reducing nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Antioxidant properties: Peppermint's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate motion sickness symptoms, such as dizziness and headaches.

Gastrointestinal soothing effects: The natural oils in peppermint capsules calm the stomach and reduce inflammation, alleviating symptoms of nausea, bloating, and discomfort often experienced during motion sickness.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Acts as a natural anxiolytic and helps reduce nausea and vomiting by blocking the neurotransmitter serotonin, which plays a key role in the development of motion sickness.
Limonene: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties and has been shown to have a positive effect on the digestive system, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Eugenol: Possesses a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that can contribute to motion sickness, and also has a calming effect on the digestive system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in menthol and menthone, which provide relief from nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Roots: They contain menthone and menthyl acetate, which help alleviate nausea and reduce dizziness.
Stems: They may contain some menthol and menthone, but are often used in combination with other parts, providing additional relief from motion sickness symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Dry 100g of fresh mentha x piperita leaves in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind 50g of dried mentha x piperita leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with 100g of vegetable cellulose powder in a ratio of 1:2.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 0.2g of the mentha x piperita powder mixture and seal them using a capsule filler.
Store the herbal mentha x piperita capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain Passiflora incarnata, a natural herb that has been traditionally used to calm the nervous system and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress. By reducing feelings of queasiness and unease, maypop capsules can help to mitigate the effects of motion sickness, allowing individuals to feel more comfortable during travel, whether by car, boat, plane, or train.
remedy_properties: Antihistaminic activity: Maypop capsules contain compounds that exhibit antihistaminic properties, which can help alleviate histamine-related symptoms such as dizziness and nausea associated with motion sickness.

Sedative and anxiolytic effects: The extracts of maypop have been shown to possess sedative and anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties, which can help reduce feelings of stress and anxiety that often exacerbate motion sickness.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Maypop capsules also contain anti-inflammatory compounds that may help alleviate the inflammatory response triggered by motion sickness, reducing symptoms such as headache, fatigue, and dizziness.
remedy_constituents: Isovitexin: Isovitexin is a flavonoid glycoside that helps alleviate motion sickness by reducing inflammation and modulating the body's physiological response to motion-induced stress.

Hesperidin: Hesperidin is a flavanone glycoside that helps with motion sickness by reducing anxiety and stress, and improving circulation, which can help alleviate nausea and dizziness associated with motion sickness.

Passifloric acid: Passifloric acid is an alkaloid that helps alleviate motion sickness by reducing inflammation, modulating neurotransmitter activity, and promoting relaxation, which can help alleviate nausea and dizziness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain a high concentration of flavonoids, particularly apigenin and kaempferol, which have anti-inflammatory and anxiolytic properties that help alleviate motion sickness symptoms.
Fruits: The pulp of Passiflora incarnata fruits is rich in flavonoids and alkaloids, which have been traditionally used to treat nausea, vomiting, and other symptoms associated with motion sickness.

Seeds: Passiflora incarnata seeds contain a higher concentration of flavonoids and alkaloids compared to the leaves and fruits, making them a popular choice for motion sickness capsules due to their potent anti-emetic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves from a trusted source.
Dry the harvested material in a low-temperature oven at 50c for 2 hours to maintain potency.
Grind the dried material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powdered passiflora incarnata with 500 milligrams of cellulose powder in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the passiflora incarnata mixture and seal them tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with motion sickness because it has a natural calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate symptoms such as dizziness and nausea. The herb contains compounds that have been shown to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, making it easier for the body to adjust to changing movements. Additionally, valerian root has been known to stimulate the brain's GABA receptors, which helps regulate balance and equilibrium, further reducing the discomfort associated with motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Sedative and anxiolytic properties: Valerian root has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as anxiety and stress.
Muscle relaxant properties: Valerian's muscle-relaxing effects can help reduce muscle tension and spasms caused by motion sickness, promoting a sense of calm and reducing discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness by reducing inflammation in the stomach and intestines.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: A sesquiterpene that exhibits antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Valerenol: A valerenic acid derivative that has shown sedative and anxiolytic effects, potentially reducing the anxiety and discomfort experienced during motion sickness.
Isovaleric acid amide: A valerenic acid amide that has shown antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may also contribute to alleviating motion sickness symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in valerenic acid, a compound that has been shown to have sedative and anti-nausea properties.
Rhyzomes: Contain valerenic acid and other compounds that help to reduce anxiety and alleviate motion sickness symptoms.
Seeds: Rich in valerenic acid and other bioactive compounds that may help to prevent and alleviate motion sickness symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried valeriana officinalis root at a temperature of 20°c to 30°c.
Grind 50g of the harvested valeriana root into a fine powder using a mill for 5 minutes.
Mix 50g of the powdered valeriana root with 50g of gelatin in a bowl.
Fill 100 empty capsules with the valeriana and gelatin mixture using a capsule filler for 15 minutes.
Label and store 100 herbal valeriana officinalis capsules in an airtight container for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with motion sickness because of its natural properties that soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing symptoms such as nausea and dizziness. The herbal remedy has a sedative effect on the stomach, calming any irritation caused by turbulent movements, and promoting relaxation in the body. As a result, chamomile capsules provide relief from the discomfort and distress associated with motion sickness, allowing individuals to feel more comfortable and composed during travel or activities that trigger the condition.
remedy_properties: Sedative and anti-anxiety properties: Chamomile's calming effects help to reduce stress and anxiety associated with motion sickness, making it easier for the body to recover from the physical symptoms.

Antihistamine-like action: Chamomile's flavonoids and terpenoids have been shown to possess antihistamine-like properties, which can help alleviate symptoms such as dizziness, nausea, and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce the inflammation and discomfort caused by motion sickness in the digestive tract, head, and neck.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: An apigenin is a flavonoid that acts as a natural antihistamine, which helps to alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness by reducing the body's histamine response and providing relief from nausea and dizziness.

Alpha-bisabolol: Alpha-bisabolol is a sesquiterpene alcohol that has anti-inflammatory and anxiolytic properties, which can help to reduce the anxiety and stress associated with motion sickness, making it easier for the body to cope with the symptoms.
Farnesene: Farnesene is a sesquiterpene that has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce the body's reaction to motion sickness, leading to a decrease in nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are commonly used to make capsules for motion sickness because of their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms.
Leaves: Leaves are used in some herbal remedies for motion sickness as they are believed to have a soothing effect on the digestive system.
Seeds: Although not as commonly used as flowers, some herbal remedies may include seeds for their potential to aid in digestion and reduce nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and clean them thoroughly with a soft brush to remove any debris.
Grind the cleaned flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder for 2-3 minutes.
Mix the powdered matricaria chamomilla with 10-20 grams of gelatin or vegetarian capsules in a bowl.
Add a small amount of water to the bowl and stir the mixture until it becomes a thick paste using a spoon for 2-3 minutes.
Fill the mixture into gelatin or vegetarian capsules using a capsule filling machine or a small spoon within 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain a natural blend of herbs that work in harmony to soothe the digestive system and calm the nervous system. The Dong quai plant, also known as Chinese angelica, has been used for centuries in traditional Chinese medicine to treat symptoms associated with motion sickness, such as dizziness, nausea, and vomiting. When taken in capsule form, its active compounds help to stabilize the body's internal environment, reducing the discomfort and disorientation caused by motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Dong quai's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and discomfort in the stomach and digestive tract, which can alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant compounds present in dong quai capsules help to neutralize free radicals that can damage the inner lining of the stomach and exacerbate symptoms of motion sickness.

Gastroprotective property: Dong quai's gastroprotective property helps to soothe and protect the digestive tract from irritation and inflammation, reducing the risk of nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain caused by motion sickness.
remedy_constituents: Coumarins: Coumarins in Angelica sinensis capsules have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate the nausea and dizziness associated with motion sickness.
Furocoumarins: Furocoumarins, specifically angelicin, have been found to have a sedative effect on the central nervous system, which may help reduce the symptoms of motion sickness.

Ligustilide: Ligustilide, a sesquiterpene in Angelica sinensis, has been reported to have a sedative and anti-emetic effect, which may help alleviate the nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Angelica sinensis are rich in berberine, which helps to alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Stems: The stems of Angelica sinensis contain volatile oils that help to calm the digestive system and reduce symptoms of motion sickness.
Leaves: The leaves of Angelica sinensis are a good source of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce the severity of motion sickness symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried angelica sinensis roots in the autumn season when the plant is mature.
Clean the roots by washing with distilled water to remove impurities and contaminants.
Dry the cleaned roots in a low-temperature oven at 50c for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to increase bioavailability.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the powder and seal the capsules tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and alkaloids, that have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to alleviate digestive issues. The capsules work by soothing the stomach and intestines, reducing inflammation and spasms caused by motion sickness. They also help to regulate blood flow and circulation, which can become disrupted during travel, further alleviating symptoms of dizziness and nausea associated with motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Improved blood flow: Ginkgo biloba may help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness by increasing blood flow to the brain and other affected areas.
Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant compounds may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by motion sickness, which can contribute to its symptoms.
Neuroprotective effects: Ginkgo biloba has been shown to have neuroprotective properties, which may help mitigate the damage caused by repeated exposure to motion stimuli.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help alleviate motion sickness by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to the symptoms of motion sickness.

Bilobalide: This terpene-like compound helps to reduce the severity of motion sickness by stabilizing mast cells and preventing the release of histamine, a chemical involved in allergic reactions and inflammation.

Ginkgolides: These unique compounds help to alleviate motion sickness by inhibiting the action of serotonin, a neurotransmitter involved in the development of motion sickness symptoms, and also by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which may help alleviate motion sickness symptoms by improving blood flow to the brain.
Seeds: They are rich in bilobalide, a compound that may help reduce motion sickness by its sedative and anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: They contain ginkgotoxin, a compound that may help alleviate motion sickness by its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves and stems with no signs of mold or mildew.
Dry the ginkgo biloba leaves and stems in a temperature-controlled environment at 50 degrees celsius for 48 hours to remove moisture.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves and stems into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Fill gelatin capsules with 400 milligrams of the ginkgo biloba powder and seal them using a capsule filler machine.
Label and store the ginkgo biloba capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain the calming properties of lavender, which has a natural ability to soothe the stomach and reduce symptoms of nausea. The capsules work by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, two common contributors to motion sickness. Additionally, the antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties of lavender may also help to alleviate any underlying gastrointestinal issues that can exacerbate motion sickness, providing relief from dizziness and discomfort during travel or other activities.
remedy_properties: Lavandulol: A sesquiterpene compound found in lavender, which has been shown to have anti-nausea and anti-emetic effects, helping to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
Linalool: Another essential oil constituent in lavender, which has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that can exacerbate motion sickness.
Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic acid present in lavender, which exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with motion sickness.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene acts as a natural anxiolytic, helping to reduce anxiety and stress associated with motion sickness.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as nausea and headaches.

Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic acid has been found to have a sedative effect on the central nervous system, which can help reduce symptoms of motion sickness by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain high amounts of volatile oils, including linalool and linalyl acetate, which help alleviate nausea and motion sickness.
Flowers: The flowers are used because they are a rich source of lavender oil, which has been traditionally used to calm the stomach and reduce motion sickness symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds are used because they contain essential oils that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with a botanical origin guarantee.
Clean the flowers by gently sifting them through a 100-micron mesh screen.
Fill a 10-milliliter gelatin capsule with 250 milligrams of the cleaned dried flowers.
Seal the capsule using a heat sealer or by applying gentle pressure.
Store the capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain a natural compound called parthenolide, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness. Additionally, feverfew's ability to relax the smooth muscle in the stomach and intestines may also contribute to its effectiveness in reducing discomfort and improving digestion during travel or other activities that trigger motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Parthenolide: Feverfew's primary bioactive compound, parthenolide has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as dizziness and nausea.

Feverfew's flavonoids: The flavonoids present in feverfew capsules have antioxidant properties that can help protect the body against oxidative stress caused by prolonged exposure to motion, reducing its negative effects on the digestive system and overall well-being.

Sesquiterpenes: Feverfew's sesquiterpenes have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antihistamine-like activities, which can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as dizziness, vertigo, and nausea by reducing inflammation and histamine release in the body.
remedy_constituents: Parthenolide: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness by reducing inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract.

Neurolide: Similar to parthenolide, neurolide is another sesquiterpene lactone that may help with motion sickness by exerting anti-inflammatory effects and potentially stabilizing the gastrointestinal tract.
Cineole: This monoterpenic ketone has a sedative effect, which may help alleviate the nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness by calming the digestive system and reducing anxiety.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and anti-emetic properties.
Flowers: They are used because of their ability to reduce nausea and vomiting, likely due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds.
Buds: They are used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, which contribute to their anti-emetic and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest tanacetum parthenium flowers and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the tanacetum parthenium flowers and leaves in a single layer at 40°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Mix 500mg of the powder with a suitable capsule filler and fill 100 capsules.
Label and package the filled capsules in a cool dry place within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Papaya capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain the enzyme papain, which breaks down proteins into smaller peptides that help to calm the stomach and reduce nausea. Additionally, the antioxidants present in papaya help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate symptoms of motion sickness, such as dizziness and lightheadedness. By reducing inflammation and soothing the digestive system, papaya capsules provide natural relief from motion sickness, making travel by car, plane, or boat a more comfortable experience.
remedy_properties: Carotenoids: Papaya's carotenoid content, particularly beta-carotene and lutein, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Vitamin C: The high vitamin C content in papaya capsules can help to reduce the severity of motion sickness symptoms by boosting the immune system and fighting off oxidative stress caused by travel-related factors.
Papain: Papain, an enzyme found in papaya, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate gastrointestinal upset, cramps, and other discomforts often experienced during motion sickness.
remedy_constituents: Papain: This proteolytic enzyme has anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.
Vitamin C (Ascorbic acid): As a natural antioxidant, vitamin C may help mitigate oxidative stress caused by motion sickness, thereby reducing symptoms such as dizziness and nausea.

Carotenoids (specifically beta-carotene): As a natural antioxidant, carotenoids may help protect against oxidative damage in the gastrointestinal tract, thereby reducing symptoms of motion sickness like nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The ripe fruit of Carica papaya is used for its anti-inflammatory and anti-emetic properties, which help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
Seeds: The seeds of Carica papaya contain papain, a proteolytic enzyme that can help reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Leaves: The leaves of Carica papaya contain compounds like isothiocyanate, which have been shown to have anti-emetic properties and can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Dry 2 kg of finely chopped carica papaya fruit in a dehydrator at 50°c for 8 hours to remove moisture.
Grind 500 grams of dried carica papaya into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 2 minutes.
Sift the powder through a 40-mesh sieve to remove lumps and ensure uniform particle size.
Fill a capsule machine with 0.5 grams of carica papaya powder and attach a capsule shell to encase the powder.
Seal the capsule and repeat the process for 1000 capsules at a production rate of 2 capsules per minute.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng capsules helps with motion sickness because of its adaptogenic properties, which enable it to help the body adapt to stressors such as changes in motion or environment. The herb contains bioactive compounds that stimulate the body's natural response to motion, reducing feelings of nausea and dizziness. Additionally, ginseng has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an upset stomach, further alleviating symptoms of motion sickness. By supporting the body's natural balance, ginseng capsules provide relief from motion sickness, allowing individuals to enjoy travel and activities without discomfort.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Ginseng has been shown to reduce stress and anxiety responses, which can exacerbate motion sickness symptoms, by regulating cortisol levels and promoting relaxation.

Antioxidant activity: Ginseng's antioxidant properties may help protect the body from oxidative damage caused by prolonged periods of motion, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with motion sickness.

Vasodilatory effects: Ginseng has been found to dilate blood vessels, which can improve blood circulation and oxygenation to the brain, helping to alleviate symptoms such as dizziness and lightheadedness often experienced during motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of ginseng are rich in bioactive compounds that help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness by reducing nausea and vomiting.
Rhyzomes: Ginseng rhyzomes contain a unique combination of saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids that work together to mitigate the effects of motion sickness and promote calmness.
remedy_recipe: Measure 250mg of dried panax ginseng root powder into a small bowl.
Weigh out 10:1 ratio of filler material such as silicon dioxide to the herb.
Mix the panax ginseng powder and filler material together thoroughly in a mortar and pestle.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal blend and seal tightly for storage.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to have anti-nausea properties. The root of the licorice plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to alleviate digestive issues, including nausea and vomiting. When taken as a supplement, herbal licorice capsules can help to soothe an upset stomach and calm the nerves, reducing the symptoms of motion sickness. By doing so, they provide natural relief from the discomfort and disorientation associated with travel-related illness.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of motion sickness by inhibiting the release of histamine, a chemical mediator involved in nausea and vomiting.
Flavonoids: Exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe the digestive system and reduce discomfort associated with motion sickness.
Triterpenoid saponins: Has a calming effect on the stomach and intestines, helping to relieve nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain caused by motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a good source of glycyrrhizin, an active compound that has anti-inflammatory and antacid properties, making it effective in alleviating symptoms of motion sickness.
Barks: The barks contain triterpenoids, which have been shown to have anti-emetic effects, helping to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted supplier or cultivate yourself ensure potency.
Grind 20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle take 10 minutes.
Mix the ground powder with 50 grams of excipients such as starch and silica in a clean glass bowl combine evenly.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared mixture using a capsule filler machine or do it manually carefully.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 12 months ensuring potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain a natural compound called eucalyptol, which has been shown to have a calming effect on the digestive system. The capsules also contain other herbal ingredients that help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, which can alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness such as nausea, dizziness, and fatigue. By promoting relaxation and soothing the stomach, Tasmanian blue gum capsules provide natural relief from motion sickness, making it easier for individuals to enjoy travel without discomfort.
remedy_properties: Cineole: A compound found in Tasmanian Blue Gum, cineole has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, helping to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Tannins: The tannins present in the plant have been shown to have a calming effect on the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by motion sickness.

Essential Oils: The essential oils found in Tasmanian Blue Gum, including cineole and other terpenoids, have natural sedative properties that can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as dizziness and disorientation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing a high concentration of eucalyptol, the roots of the Tasmanian Blue Gum plant have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate nausea and dizziness associated with motion sickness.

Barks: The barks of the plant contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-nausea effects, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal capsules for motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator or oven on low heat for six hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind dried eucalyptus globulus leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for efficient extraction.
Mix one teaspoon of powdered eucalyptus globulus with 100ml of boiling water in a glass container for ten minutes of infusion time to create a concentrated tincture.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the cooled tincture mixture at a dosage of 250mg per capsule and store in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain extracts from Vitex agnus-castus, a herb that has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalance. Research suggests that chaste tree can help regulate the body's response to motion and reduce feelings of nausea and dizziness caused by motion sickness. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may help soothe the stomach and digestive tract, further reducing discomfort associated with travel-related motion sickness. By taking chaste tree capsules before traveling, individuals can enjoy a smoother ride and reduced symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Vasodilation: Chasteberry's ability to dilate blood vessels helps alleviate symptoms of motion sickness by increasing blood flow and reducing constriction in the hands and feet, which can become cold and sweaty during episodes.

Serotonin regulation: Chaste tree's influence on serotonin levels in the body may help stabilize mood and reduce anxiety, which are common triggers for motion sickness, allowing individuals to better cope with symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Chasteberry's anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate nausea, dizziness, and stomach discomfort associated with motion sickness by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chaste tree roots are used to make capsules for motion sickness due to their high content of essential oils and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
Seeds: Chaste tree seeds are used in capsule form to treat motion sickness because they contain a compound called casticin, which has been shown to have anti-nausea effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh vitex agnus-castus leaves and flowers from a trusted supplier or grow your own in well-drained soil.
Cleanse the harvested herbs by rinsing them gently with distilled water and air-drying for 24 hours.
Prepare the dried herbs by grinding them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle to ensure uniformity.
Mix 400mg of the ground vitex agnus-castus powder with 100mg of gelatin and 50mg of silica in a small bowl, then fill empty capsules with the mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or freeze for longer shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha capsules helps with motion sickness because of its natural adaptogenic properties, which enable it to reduce stress and anxiety triggered by motion. The capsules contain extracts from the Withania somnifera plant, also known as Ashwagandha, which has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to alleviate symptoms of nausea, dizziness, and vomiting associated with motion sickness. By calming the nervous system and reducing inflammation, ashwagandha capsules can effectively alleviate symptoms of motion sickness, allowing individuals to travel or engage in activities without discomfort.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Ashwagandha helps the body adapt to stress and anxiety caused by motion sickness, reducing symptoms such as nausea and dizziness.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Ashwagandha's anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate the gastrointestinal discomfort and inflammation associated with motion sickness.

GABA-enhancing properties: Ashwagandha contains compounds that increase GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) levels in the brain, which can help regulate nausea and vomiting responses triggered by motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of bioactive compounds, including withanolides and alkaloids, which have been shown to effectively alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
Barks: The bark contains triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried ashwagandha roots with a minimum of 3% withanolides from reputable sources for a total quantity of 250 grams.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder for 10 minutes.
Sift the powdered root through a 100-mesh sieve to remove any lumps or large particles for 5 minutes.
Fill #0 capsules with a mixture of 500 milligrams of the sifted ashwagandha powder and 250 milligrams of gelatin for 20 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in a cool, dry place for at least two weeks to allow the ingredients to mature before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain curcumin, a potent bioactive compound that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin has been shown to alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness by reducing inflammation in the gut and brain. Additionally, turmeric's natural antibacterial and antifungal properties help to combat any underlying infections that may exacerbate motion sickness. By taking turmeric capsules before traveling or engaging in activities that trigger motion sickness, individuals can experience relief from symptoms and enjoy a smoother ride.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Turmeric's active compound curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness by blocking prostaglandins, hormones involved in inflammation and pain.

Curcuminoids: The combination of curcuminoids (including demethoxycurcumin and bisdemethoxycurcumin) in turmeric capsules has been shown to possess anti-nausea properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness by reducing the activity of the neurotransmitter serotonin, a key player in nausea and vomiting.

Volatiles: Turmeric contains volatile compounds like turmerone and atlantone, which have been found to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, potentially helping to reduce the discomfort and pain associated with motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcuminoids, the Rhyzomes are the primary source of turmeric's medicinal properties and are effective against nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Roots: The Roots contain a higher concentration of bioactive compounds than other parts, making them ideal for extracting curcuminoids and other beneficial compounds to alleviate motion sickness symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality turmeric roots measuring one hundred grams of fresh or dried material.
Dry the turmeric roots in a warm, dry place at room temperature for seven days.
Grind the dried turmeric roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder for five minutes.
Mix the turmeric powder with 500 milligrams of rice flour and 50 milliliters of water to create a dough.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with one hundred milligrams of the turmeric-dough mixture and store in an airtight container for six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain the herb salvia officinalis, which has a natural calming effect on the digestive system. When taken before traveling by car, boat, plane, or other means, sage capsules can help to soothe and regulate stomach muscles, reducing symptoms of nausea and dizziness associated with motion sickness. By stabilizing the digestive process, sage capsules can provide relief from discomfort and allow individuals to enjoy their journey without interruption.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine-like compounds: Sage contains antihistamine-like compounds that help to counteract the effects of histamine, a chemical released during motion sickness, which can cause symptoms like nausea and vomiting.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can contribute to motion sickness symptoms such as nausea and abdominal discomfort.

Digestive soothing compounds: The compounds present in sage capsules, including rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, have been shown to soothe the digestive tract and relax the muscles of the stomach, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: With a high concentration of essential oils, rhyzomes possess antispasmodic and carminative effects, helping to ease stomach discomfort associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves from your garden or purchase from a reputable supplier weighing approximately 100 grams.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves by spreading them on paper towels for at least 24 hours before grinding into a fine powder.
Combine the ground salvia officinalis leaves with 50 grams of gelatin in a blender and blend until well mixed and powdered.
Fill empty capsules with approximately 250 milligrams of the herbal mixture per capsule using a capsule filler machine or scoop and pour method.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to one year or refrigerate for longer storage life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain a natural compound called thymol, which has been shown to have anti-nausea properties. When taken before traveling, the capsules can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as dizziness and vomiting. The herbal extract works by relaxing the stomach muscles, reducing nausea, and improving digestion, allowing individuals to travel with greater ease and comfort. As a result, thyme capsules have become a popular natural remedy for motion sickness sufferers.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Thyme's antispasmodic compounds, such as thymol, help relax muscle spasms and cramps in the stomach and digestive tract, which can alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Thyme's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and terpenes, may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body caused by motion sickness, which can contribute to symptoms such as dizziness and discomfort.

Gastroprotective properties: Thyme's gastroprotective compounds, including thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to protect the stomach lining from irritation and damage caused by motion sickness, reducing the risk of gastric ulcers and bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have been shown to possess anti-nausea and anti-inflammatory properties, making them a popular choice for alleviating motion sickness symptoms.

Leaves: Thyme leaves contain a high concentration of essential oils, including thymol and carvacrol, which are known for their natural calming effects on the stomach and can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves from a trusted source ensuring potency.
Dry the harvested thymus vulgaris leaves using shade or low-temperature drying for 3 days.
Grind the dried thymus vulgaris leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 500mg of ground thymus vulgaris powder with 250ml of distilled water to create a tincture solution taking 10 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250mg of the tincture solution and seal immediately readying for consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maca capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain Peruvian ginseng, which has been traditionally used to alleviate nausea and dizziness. The herb's natural adaptogenic properties help to stabilize the body's balance and reduce stress on the digestive system, allowing individuals to better cope with the sensory conflicts that trigger motion sickness. Additionally, maca's anti-inflammatory compounds may also help to soothe the stomach and reduce discomfort associated with queasiness. By promoting a sense of calm and reducing symptoms, maca capsules can provide relief from the distressing effects of motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: Maca helps the body adapt to physical and environmental stressors, including changes in motion, by regulating cortisol levels and promoting a sense of balance and stability.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Maca's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to symptoms of motion sickness such as nausea and stomach discomfort.

Vasodilatory Properties: Maca's ability to dilate blood vessels may help improve circulation and reduce blood pressure fluctuations that can exacerbate motion sickness symptoms like dizziness and lightheadedness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in starch and fiber, which helps to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness by stabilizing the digestive system.
Rhyzomes: Contains bioactive compounds that have been shown to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Barks: Has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the stomach, reducing discomfort caused by motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried lepidium meyenii powder from a reputable supplier in 250mg increments.
Mix the lepidium meyenii powder with 500mg of silica gel powder and 100mg of magnesium stearate in a small bowl.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with approximately 750mg of the mixed powder using an encapsulation machine.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.
Take two capsules 30 minutes prior to travel and as needed while experiencing motion sickness.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to motion sickness that are treated with herbal capsules are vertigo, labyrinthitis, and Benign Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo (BPPV). These conditions cause dizziness, nausea, and balance issues. Herbal supplements like ginger, passionflower, and vestibular sedatives have been found to alleviate these symptoms by reducing inflammation, calming the nervous system, and improving blood flow to the inner ear.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with motion sickness is a blend of ginger, peony, and green tea. Ginger capsules have natural anti-inflammatory properties that reduce nausea and vomiting, while peony capsules help to stabilize the nervous system and prevent dizziness. Green tea capsules, rich in antioxidants, also aid in reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, making this combination an effective and holistic remedy for motion sickness. Regular use can provide long-term relief and comfort during travel.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. This helps to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness. Ginger has been traditionally used to alleviate digestive issues and is known for its ability to regulate digestive enzymes and improve stomach health. By taking Zingiber officinale capsules, individuals can experience relief from motion sickness symptoms, allowing them to travel and engage in activities without discomfort. This natural remedy offers a convenient solution for those prone to motion sickness.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness by reducing inflammation in the stomach.
Shogaols: They have a strong effect on the digestive system, helping to calm the stomach and reduce nausea, which can be triggered by motion sickness.

Volatile oils (mainly containing zingiberene): These oils have a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to regulate the stomach and alleviate symptoms such as nausea and vomiting, making them beneficial in treating motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhizomes are the most used part of Zingiber officinale to make capsules for motion sickness because they contain the highest concentration of active compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, responsible for its medicinal properties.
Roots: Roots are used as an alternative to rhyzomes due to their similar composition, which helps in alleviating nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Stems: Stems of Zingiber officinale are also used in some supplements, although in lower concentrations, to provide some anti-inflammatory properties that can aid in reducing motion sickness symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the zingiber officinale roots in the early morning when the dew is still present on the surface.
Clean the zingiber officinale roots thoroughly with water to remove any dirt or debris from the surface.
Dry the zingiber officinale roots in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried zingiber officinale roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder with a 1/4 cup dosage.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 1/4 teaspoon of the zingiber officinale powder and seal the capsules immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with motion sickness because they improve blood flow to the brain, reducing the severity of symptoms. The active ingredients in ginkgo biloba, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce nausea and dizziness. Additionally, ginkgo biloba has been shown to improve cognitive function and reduce stress, which can exacerbate motion sickness. By promoting a sense of calm and balance, ginkgo biloba capsules can provide relief from the discomfort of motion sickness.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help alleviate motion sickness by reducing inflammation, improving blood flow, and stabilizing blood vessel walls, which can help minimize the effects of inner ear disturbances.
Terpenoids: Assist in reducing motion sickness by having anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help counteract the physical symptoms of nausea and dizziness.
Bilobalide: Contribute to reducing motion sickness by acting as a neuroprotective agent, which can help calm the nervous system and alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Ginkgo biloba leaves are the most commonly used part for motion sickness capsules due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which help alleviate symptoms.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are also used to make motion sickness capsules, as they contain a compound called bilobalide, which has been shown to have anti-nausea properties.
Leaves: Some motion sickness capsules may also include dried and processed ginkgo biloba leaves, which are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, and are believed to help reduce nausea and vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of high-quality ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees during autumn season.
Dry the leaves in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours to preserve potency.
Grind 50g of dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the powder with 50g of gelatin to create a uniform blend for encapsulation.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the blend and seal them immediately to preserve freshness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain a natural extract that promotes relaxation and calms the nervous system. The active compounds in Passiflora incarnata, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, work to reduce anxiety and stress, making it easier to manage the symptoms of motion sickness. By promoting a sense of calm and reducing the physical symptoms of nausea and dizziness, Passiflora incarnata capsules provide a natural solution to alleviate the discomfort associated with motion sickness. This natural remedy is particularly useful for those who experience frequent or severe motion sickness.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce the severity of motion sickness by mitigating oxidative stress and inflammation associated with nausea and vomiting.

Alkaloids: These constituents, particularly passiflorine, have sedative and anxiolytic effects, which may help alleviate the anxiety and stress that contribute to motion sickness, ultimately reducing nausea and vomiting.
Glycosides: These compounds may help regulate the gut and balance the body's internal environment, which can help alleviate motion sickness symptoms, including nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are commonly used for making capsules for motion sickness due to their high content of passifloric acid, which helps to alleviate nausea.
Fruits: Fruits are used due to their high concentration of passifloric acid, a compound that has been traditionally used to treat motion sickness.
Roots: Roots are used to make capsules for motion sickness because they contain a high amount of alkaloids, which are believed to have antiemetic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves from a trusted source in sufficient quantities.
Grind the dried flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the powder with a natural capsule filler in a ratio of 1:1 or 1:2 for proper dosing.
Fill empty herbal capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filler machine within 10 minutes.
Label and store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain the extract of oats, which has a calming effect on the nervous system. This helps to reduce anxiety and stress associated with travel and motion. The anti-inflammatory properties in Avena sativa also aid in soothing digestive issues, a common cause of motion sickness. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, Avena sativa capsules provide relief from the symptoms of motion sickness, making travel and travel-related activities more comfortable.
remedy_constituents: Avenacosides: These saponins help alleviate motion sickness by reducing inflammation and stabilizing the stomach, which in turn reduces the severity of nausea and vomiting.
Isovitexin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help calm the digestive system and reduce the symptoms of motion sickness, such as nausea and dizziness.
Avenanthramides: These phenolic compounds have a calming effect on the digestive system and can help reduce inflammation, which can contribute to the development of motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are rich in a compound called avenalin, which is believed to help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Roots: The roots of Avena sativa contain avenacosides, which are thought to have anti-emetic properties that can help combat motion sickness.
Leaves: The leaves contain a compound called avenanthramides, which may help alleviate nausea and vomiting by reducing inflammation and stress on the digestive system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25 grams of avena sativa roots and stems at dawn when the plant is at its highest moisture content.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer at 40°c for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried avena sativa into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder.
Mix 3 grams of the ground avena sativa powder with 1 gram of cellulose powder to create a uniform blend.
Fill 50-count gelatin capsules with the uniform blend and seal them to create herbal avena sativa capsules.

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper nigrum capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain the extract of black pepper, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that aid in calming the digestive system. The bioactive compounds present in black pepper, such as piperine and beta-caryophyllene, have been shown to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness. Additionally, piperine has been found to improve blood circulation and reduce oxidative stress, further contributing to its anti-motion sickness properties. This natural remedy can provide relief from motion sickness without the need for pharmaceuticals.
remedy_constituents: Piperine: Helps alleviate motion sickness by inhibiting the activity of 5-HT3 receptors in the stomach and intestines, thus reducing nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Terpenes (including Caryophyllene and Pinene): Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties and helps reduce nausea by interacting with the body's natural defense mechanisms against motion sickness.
Flavonoids (including Kaempferol and Quercetin): Possesses antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and alleviate symptoms of motion sickness, including nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Piper nigrum seeds are commonly used to make capsules for motion sickness due to their high concentration of piperine, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.

Leaves: Piper nigrum leaves are another part used to make capsules for motion sickness, as they contain compounds like piperine and lignans that have been shown to have anti-nausea and anti-inflammatory effects.

Fruits: Piper nigrum fruits, specifically the peppercorns, are also used to make capsules for motion sickness, as they contain piperine and other bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to alleviate nausea and vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried piper nigrum fruit at peak ripeness for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested piper nigrum fruit in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried piper nigrum fruit into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 2g of the powdered piper nigrum fruit with 1g of a natural binder such as gum arabic.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with the piper nigrum powder mixture and seal them immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain quinine, a natural compound that has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of nausea and dizziness. The active compound in Cinchona officinalis, quinine, has anti-inflammatory and anti-emetic properties, which help to reduce the severity of motion sickness. By reducing inflammation and stabilizing blood pressure, quinine helps to alleviate the unpleasant symptoms associated with motion sickness, making travel and transportation more enjoyable. This natural remedy offers a safe and effective solution for those prone to motion sickness.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: Quinine helps alleviate motion sickness by acting as a natural antihistamine and anti-inflammatory agent, which reduces nausea and vomiting symptoms.
Quinidine: Quinidine helps with motion sickness by exhibiting anti-adrenergic properties, which counteract the effects of stress hormones that contribute to nausea and vomiting.
Cinchonidine: Cinchonidine helps alleviate motion sickness by acting as a natural calcium channel blocker, which can help reduce muscle spasms and alleviate nausea and vomiting symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the primary source of quinine, a key ingredient in treating motion sickness.
Barks: The barks of the Cinchona tree contain quinine and other alkaloids that help alleviate motion sickness symptoms.
Stems: The stems of the Cinchona tree also contain quinine and other alkaloids that contribute to the effectiveness of motion sickness capsules.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cinchona officinalis leaves and bark from mature trees grown in a controlled environment.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 60°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 10 grams of the powder with 20 grams of gelatin and 10 grams of water to create a uniform mixture.
Encapsulate the mixture in size 0 capsules using a manual capsule machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain an active compound called anethole, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. When ingested, anethole helps to calm the stomach and reduce nausea and dizziness caused by motion. The capsules also help to relax the muscles and improve digestion, reducing the symptoms of motion sickness. As a result, Foeniculum vulgare capsules provide a natural and effective remedy for motion sickness, making them a popular choice for travelers and individuals prone to motion sickness.
remedy_constituents: Foeniculin: This terpene helps with motion sickness by exhibiting antiemetic properties, reducing nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Anethole: This phenylpropene helps with motion sickness by inhibiting acetylcholinesterase, an enzyme that breaks down acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter involved in nausea and vomiting.
Sulphur-containing compounds: These compounds help with motion sickness by exhibiting carminative properties, reducing gas and bloating that can exacerbate nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used to make capsules for motion sickness because they contain a compound called anethole, which has been shown to have anti-nausea properties.
Leaves: They are used to make capsules for motion sickness because they contain a high concentration of essential oils, including anethole, which helps to alleviate nausea and dizziness.
Stems: They are used to make capsules for motion sickness because they contain a combination of essential oils and flavonoids that have been found to have anti-inflammatory and anti-nausea effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare flowers and seeds from mature plants in late summer when they are fully ripe.
Dry the harvested foeniculum vulgare flowers and seeds in a well-ventilated area for 1-2 weeks.
Grind 10-20 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare flowers and seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with a suitable capsule filler and fill pre-made gelatin capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the mixture.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Centella asiatica capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain compounds like triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which are often associated with motion sickness. Additionally, Centella asiatica is known to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness such as dizziness and nausea. This natural remedy can provide relief from motion sickness without the need for harsh medications.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties and help reduce nausea and vomiting by modulating the body's response to motion.
Flavonoids: They have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness, including dizziness and nausea.
Asiatic acid: This triterpenoid has anti-emetic properties, which help prevent vomiting and alleviate nausea, making it beneficial for individuals experiencing motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make capsules for motion sickness because they contain compounds like triterpenoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and adaptogenic properties that help alleviate nausea and dizziness.

Stems: They are used to make capsules for motion sickness because they contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce symptoms of motion sickness.

Roots: They are used to make capsules for motion sickness because they contain bioactive compounds like triterpenoids and saponins, which have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and reduce nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh centella asiatica leaves and flowers and clean them thoroughly with water.
Dry the centella asiatica leaves and flowers in a shaded area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Powder 300g of the dried centella asiatica leaves and flowers using a high-speed blender or mill to create a fine powder.
Mix 200g of the centella asiatica powder with 100g of gelatin powder and 50g of tapioca starch in a bowl.
Fill the mixture into empty gelatin capsules and seal them tightly for 30 minutes to set the ingredients.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain licorice root extract, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the stomach and alleviate nausea. The compounds present in Glycyrrhiza glabra, such as glycyrrhizin, have been shown to stabilize the digestive system and reduce the symptoms of motion sickness. Additionally, licorice root has been traditionally used to calm the stomach and promote relaxation, making it a popular natural remedy for motion sickness and related digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help with motion sickness by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which are contributing factors to motion sickness symptoms.
Terpenoids: They have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate motion sickness symptoms by reducing nausea and vomiting.
Glycyrrhizin: It helps with motion sickness by stabilizing stomach acid and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part, as they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Leaves: The leaves also contain some glycyrrhizin and other compounds that may help in reducing motion sickness symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain smaller amounts of glycyrrhizin and may also be used, although less frequently, to make capsules for motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and sift them through a fine mesh to remove impurities.
Grind the sifted roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of the powdered glycyrrhiza glabra with 30 grams of gelatin in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them using a capsule sealing device.
Label and date the capsules and store them in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Uncaria rhynchophylla capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain the active compound rhynchophylline, which has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to alleviate nausea and vomiting. Studies have shown that rhynchophylline can help to reduce the frequency and severity of motion sickness symptoms by blocking the neurotransmitter serotonin, which is involved in the body's response to motion. As a result, Uncaria rhynchophylla capsules can provide relief from motion sickness and allow individuals to travel with greater comfort and ease.
remedy_constituents: Rhynchophylline: This alkaloid is believed to have anti-emetic properties, helping to alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Uncarinatine: This alkaloid is thought to have a stabilizing effect on the inner ear, which is responsible for balance and equilibrium, thus reducing the severity of motion sickness symptoms.
Isorhynchophylline: This alkaloid has been reported to have a sedative effect, which can help to reduce the anxiety and stress associated with motion sickness, thereby alleviating symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Uncaria rhynchophylla are used to make capsules for motion sickness because they contain alkaloids, which help alleviate nausea and vomiting.
Leaves: The leaves of Uncaria rhynchophylla are used to make capsules for motion sickness because they also contain alkaloids that have anti-nausea properties.
Barks: The barks of Uncaria rhynchophylla are used to make capsules for motion sickness because they contain similar alkaloids that help reduce the symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of uncaria rhynchophylla roots and clean them thoroughly with water to remove impurities and debris.
Dry the cleaned uncaria rhynchophylla roots in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 6 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind 10g of dried uncaria rhynchophylla roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground powder with 20g of cellulose powder and 5g of magnesium stearate in a stainless steel bowl.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a powder filling machine with a dosage of 500mg per capsule.
